rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 2, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC's motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is granted pursuant to C.C.P. sections 583.310 and 583.360 (mandatory dismissal) and 583.410 and 583.420 (discretionary dismissal). Defendant is dismissed from this action. Defendant's second amended proof of service demonstrates that it properly served Edward Woody, the sole plaintiff, with its motion with sufficient notice and no opposition was filed. Hearing required unless defendant informs Mr. Woody of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES OWEN et al VS. ASBESTOS DFDTS.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant Washington Group International, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 2, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Washington Group International, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment will be continued pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. section 437c(h) request, which is granted. A hearing is required to discuss a new hearing date and briefing schedule. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR POPE ET AL VS. KAISER GYPSUN COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Proposed Intervenors (On Behalf Of Their Insured Defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) For An Order Granting Leave To File A Complaint-In-Intervention Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 387
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 2, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Proposed Intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Company, et al.'s motion for an order granting leave to file a complaint-in-intervention pursuant to C.C.P. section 387 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
